title: Scatterplot: focus on region
question: When I try to focus on a specific region in a scatterplot in the catalysis app, the filter does not get updated in the same way as I can see it does in the production of the Solar Cells app. Both regarding the update of the range in the window but also regarding the displayed entry list. I think this is related to the repeating subsections and array quantities, but I am not sure if this is the intended behaviour or a bug. Can I tests this in the Oasis? Which scatter plot?
proposed_answer: Note that when you select a region in the scatter plot, you are essentially setting a filter like `500 K < temperature < 600 K AND 50 < conversion < 80`. If `temperature` is an array, this will include entries where temperature is e.g. [300, 400, 500]. But maybe first let me know how to reproduce this and then we can see if there is indeed a bug, or if the behaviour is simply unexpected and should be reconsidered. Yes, you can test this in the[Test Oasis](https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/oasis/gui/search/heterogeneouscatalyst?results.properties.catalytic.reaction.reaction_conditions.temperature%5Bgte%5D=%20K&results.properties.catalytic.reaction.reaction_conditions.temperature%5Blte%5D=%20K) it does show the first filter, e.g. for the temperature, but not the other axis, e.g. the feed composition, it also does not update the range that it shows in the widget. partly it is because an entry that falls within the search window also has data points outside.

title: Dataset DOI link -> DOI NOT FOUND
question: I will have a look at those. I am sorry that this happens again and again. It seems to turn into a regular problem and maybe we need to improve our error handling. Basically datacite.org does not work, but we dont tell the user and then they are confused. I think, I have a script to check for broken DOIs. I will have a look at this tomorrow. Are there some updates here? is it possible to get a registered doi on an oasis?
proposed_answer: Here's another dead DOI link from https://dx.doi.org//NOMAD/-1 I will look at the dead DOIs. The "DOIs from Oasis" is a different topic. To create a DOI, you need an account with datacite (or another company that does this) to use their APIs to create DOIs. There are fees associated etc. We only have this for the central NOMAD. I don't think, its a good idea or even legally allowed to share the account credentials with Oases. ok thanks this is good to know, i think then for oasis the create doi feature should be disabled then?

title: Dataset DOI link -> DOI NOT FOUND

id: Issues with NOMAD-Sintering Tutorial and NOMAD Oasis Docker Configuration|2024-06-10T11:05:16.439000Z|a0001b31a3|q9-a10
title: Issues with NOMAD-Sintering Tutorial and NOMAD Oasis Docker Configuration
question: Not sure what you mean with the "docker compose" vs the "docker-compose"? `docker compose` is the command that should be used to run the docker image as defined in the `docker-compose.yaml` config file: https://docs.docker.com/reference/cli/docker/compose/ older version of docker uses `docker-compose` to run docker config files, but recent versions use `docker compose` command How can we determine which plugin to use when reading a file and how do we navigate graphs on NOMAD?
proposed_answer: I'll try to provide some answers for the two questions: 1. What do you mean by "reading a file"? Do you mean when you upload a file to NOMAD? What parser gets called is decide by the matching criteria. The simplest criteria is to match on either the file name/ending or the mime type of the file using the `MatchingParser` class. You can find more information about this in the docs here: https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/docs/explanation/processing.html#matching 2. Not sure exactly what you mean by graphs. NOMAD doesn't have a graph backend but we do have an option of finding the back references between entries. These are resolved in the GUI so to navigate in a graphical sense you can follow the references where they are place in the data section and the back references can all be found on the overview page. As far as I know we have no API endpoint for querying across multiple references, this would have to be done on the client side. Maybe if you provide some more information on what you want to do I could provide more information.
thread_id: Issues with NOMAD-Sintering Tutorial and NOMAD Oasis Docker Configuration|2024-06-10T11:05:16.439000Z|a0001b31a3

title: NotImplementedError for context.raw_path_exists method
question: When running nomad installation with app_worker, the `m_context` is an instance of the class `nomad.datamodel.context.ServerContext` which has a well-defined `raw_path_exists` method. But while running pytests, which uses the nomad as a package (and not the local installation), `m_context` is an instance of class `nomad.datamodel.context.Context` in which `raw_path_exists` is not implemented. Because of this difference, the pytests fail during the plugin testing, when using `archive.m_context.raw_path_exists(file_path)`. Can we harmonize these two methods for the classes `ServerContext` and `C …
proposed_answer: Clarification: when using pytest, `m_context` is of class `nomad.datamodel.context.ClientContext` which inherits `nomad.datamode.context.Context`, and as the child doesn't have its own `raw_path_exists` method, it defaults to the one from the parent, which is not implemented. Something like this could resolve it https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/commit/2e8d24cd139cef1a9b9b7244c25c00b4333f28f1 I guess we only implemented the Context interface based on what was necessary. The ServerContext is the more complete one as this is used all the time in the app. The ClientContext is only used by, well, clients. E.g. when you do the "nomad parse" command, or the ArchiveQuery instantiates metainfo object froms the retrieved data. For the first case "nomad parse" you commit seems ok. For the second one, it does not hurt, but it also does not help, as the raw data is most likely not locally available. I guess it complements the existing `raw..` functions. Feel free to create an MR and merge this.

title: Recreating deleted upload on OASIS
question: Do we need to restore the mongodb if we want to reintroduce a deleted upload? Or would it be enough to reintroduce the folder in the .volumes and trigger a reprocessing from the CLI?
proposed_answer: Depends: MongoDB does contain information that cannot be recreated by simply reprocessing (upload id, entry id, timestamps, authors, coauthors, inclusion in datasets, etc). If you need to restore this, you will need the MongoDB snapshot. But if you don't care, you can simply reprocess. Hrmm, okay, I guess the main issue is if there are references to this upload and we then create a new one. Is there a way to recreate and upload with a specified ID? The way to recreate everythign with an existing ID would be to move the correctly named folder into .volumes, re-add the data (uploads + entries) into MongoDB from a backup, and run `nomad admin uploads index <upload-id>` to get ES populated with data about the entries.

title: Create multiple entry from parser or new entry in normalizer
question: class MyMatchingParser(MatchingParser): def parse( self, mainfile: str, archive: 'EntryArchive', logger: 'BoundLogger', child_archives: dict[str, 'EntryArchive'] = None, ) -> None: with open(mainfile) as file: childern = yaml.load(file)['childern'] entry_data = ParentArchiveEntryData() ... # set up entry_data here child_entry_archives = [] for child_aux_file in childern: with open(child_aux_file) as child_file: child = yaml.load(child_file) with archive.m_context.update_entry( f'{child['name']}.json', process=True, write=True ) as content_dict: child_archive = EntryArchive() ... # set up child …
proposed_answer: If you don't need any ELN functionality you should be able to use a parser and `child_archives`. However, I have never done this myself so I would then refer you to someone from Area C/D. Perhaps could explain what's going wrong there. However if you do want to have an ELN you need to have a mainfile ending with **`.archive`**`.json/yaml` for **each** ELN entry. So for that you can't (as far as I know) use the `child_archives`. Maybe you first let me know about the ELN needs and then I (or Alvin) can give you more answers after that. You can implement the iis_mainfile method of your parser to conditionally create the child_archives based on the auxiliary files present. Then the parse method can take child_archives as argument in addtion to mainfile, archive and logger The child archives is a dict of key:archive pairs where the keys are the list returned by is_mainfile. In your parse function you can then reference your archives through one of the child archives through the workflow2 section. see the docs here https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/docs/explanation/processing.html

title: Pydantic validation errors in QueryEditQuantity
question: - mainfile of the `first_processing` entry - main file of the `second_processing` entry: and I were trying to debug. Maybe can help us identify the issue?
proposed_answer: So the validation done [here](https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/blob/develop/nomad/datamodel/data.py?ref_type=heads#L267) is failing. Fixing this would be a matter of tracking down which part of the code is modifyig the query in the second processing run: My guess would be that the `model_validata(value).model_dump()` is creating this malformed version during the first processing. I found the issue. In the models for operators, the name of the field is `op` while the actual name of the operator is defined as an alias (for example [here](https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/blob/develop/nomad/app/v1/models/models.py?ref_type=heads#L118) ). When `model_dump()` is used, it defaults to using `op` as the key. Setting `model_dump(by_alias=True)` fixes the issue

question: for this analysis, this is really helpful. I don't think this is an issue with hardware. I think that we can optimize this part you pointed out, especially in the case of scatter plots. The scatter plots only typically require 2/3 search quantities, so I hope we can skip most of the other search quantities in that loop just by looking at the required fields that are already sent in the request. Let me create an issue out of this and I see what we can do. That makes sense, that can probably also speed up other widgets, as for histograms/terms you even only need one search quantity, no?
proposed_answer: In the case of histogram/terms the code is not even looking at the search quantity values directly, but we instead use the internal aggregation calls from ES. So these widgets don't have the same performance problem. This indeed seems to help a lot. I drafted this solution here as a starting point: https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/merge_requests/2408 I also saw that the searched quantities are in principle contained in doc_type.quantities, however with a different schema according to the application definition, e.g. `pynxtools.nomad.schema.Mpes`, rather than `pynxtools.nomad.schema.Root`. I assume this could then only be used by searching just within one appdef, right? With this fix, scatter plots are actually updating faster now than histograms or terms.

id: Publishing an entry from Oasis to Central NOMAD|2024-08-29T07:38:20.591000Z|c039d98db7|q25-a26
title: Publishing an entry from Oasis to Central NOMAD
question: I think I have part of the answer to it but I prefer to discuss this. As suggested by the third point in the 'Current Limitations' section of the documentation mentioned above, only a metadata transfer could happen at some point, to avoid having to move big raw files. I guess that to do this would be easier if the metadata sections concerned have the same "environment" (the same available metadata fields) between both the Oasis and the central NOMAD, for instance, the `results` section. However, when it comes to having a new parser in the Oasis, once the parsing is made, the resulting entry al …
proposed_answer: Part of my answer with my understanding: Even if new metadata fields can be integrated in an Oasis through the bespoke schemas and parsers, they don't exist in the central NOMAD and so it is not just a matter of being written in the NOMAD Metainfo language to be able to go the central NOMAD but in addition, the central NOMAD must already have these metadata fields available somewhere (just like the `results` section). id: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing|2024-07-12T10:18:27.642000Z|c6b7f00525|q6-a7
title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
question: and lines below have the exact logic... 1 - symmetry is completely ignored, parsers output only the irreducible list of k-point into `k_mesh.points`. For example OpenMX doesn't set method.k_mesh, but in eigenvalues sets the `k_mesh.points` (the values are actually printed only for irreducible k-points). We don't set either multiplicities nor weights (because the code doesn't print them), but I can add them. OpenMX only uses the inversion symmetry, so multiplicity is 1 for Gamma and 2 for anything else (at least for non-collinear non-spinorbit). Therefore in practice https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de …
proposed_answer: 3 - if the k_mesh.grid is provided but not the points, the points will be reconstructed (at least for Gamma-centered mesh) https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/blob/develop/nomad/normalizing/method.py?ref_type=heads#L378 `points = np.meshgrid(*[np.linspace(0, 1, n) for n in k_mesh.grid])`. This however will create the full grid, so again will be inconsistent with the parsers which provide the symmetry reduced ones into `points` , so IMO we should save this one into `all_points` and duplicate them into points only in case of P1 symmetry. 4 - https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/blob/develop/nomad/normalizing/method.py?ref_type=heads#L370 k_mesh.dimensionality will be 3 for all of 1x1x1, 1x1x6, and 20x20x1 grids... is this expected and it is the way the metainfo definition is intended? What I think we should do (we can take a stab at some of this, but at this point I'm looking for a agreement on prefered way to move forward here): Only construct the `k_mesh.grid` if either `all_points` or `points` and one of `multiplicities` or `weights` are provided (or points are provided and all mupltiplicities are one).

title: Metainfo Browser for Schema Plugins
question: Is there any existing plans to have something like the Metainfo Browser that can be launched directly from a plugin? and I were discussing this morning, and think this would fullfill a long-time desire to be able to browse domain-specific Metainfo structures. The normal metainfo browser should show plugins as a "source" when installed. But I guess you want something, where you don't have a nomad installation and just get a browser, e.g. `nomad admin run metainfo-browser`?
proposed_answer: yeah, something like that. The main case we had in mind is that a user wants to understand the schema structure that is included within a plugin, e.g., from the standard simulation schema used in the central NOMAD (this is linked to how we should add the new schemas that we are developing to the corresponding plugin docs, which is why the question was initially raised). But this then generalizes to a tool where anyone can visualize the schema that they are building, which is nice. In the past, we were always talking about how to point users to the sections/quantities of the metainfo that are actually relevant to their domain. With the move to schema plugins this is now straightforward with such a tool For install plugins you can use the normal metainfo browser, e.g. here is the `nomad_measurments` on the example oasis: <https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/oasis/gui/analyze/metainfo/nomad_measurements>. Maybe we can improve the metainfo browser, but there is no plan for an external tool/service.

title: displaying non-symmetric tensors in GUI
question: In the case of a 1D array, there is no change in how the actual value is accessed. However, for 2D arrays, setting invert effectively transposes the matrix by swapping rowIndex and columnIndex when accessing values. invert only affects how the data is visualized, not the actual stored data. The decision to apply invert is determined by the schema. different theories and methods may represent tensors differently very much! What would be then the best practice for the use cases, in which we wish to skip transposing the tensors in the GUI, because we need exactly the tensor in the archive and sho …
proposed_answer: e.g. : if we let the parser transpose the data as `Matrix.T`, GUI displays the data correctly ✅ , but the stored data in the archive is now transposed ⛔ if we let the parser parse the data as `Matrix`, the stored data is fine ✅ , but GUI implies otherwise ⛔ In general I'm really not sure why the UI should transpose the data: I guess it is a matter of how one is used to seeing the data displayed and stored (row-major order and column-major order). and the original person who implemented the visualization (Markus?) made a choice.

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
question: In principle what you suggest is doable, but we would need to investigate quite a lot of time to properly check and ensure that this flag correctly protects all of the endpoints etc. So I would say that at this point we are not willing to implement this, unless we hear from multiple sources that this would be a necessary feature. I would encourage trying out other solutions for now: restrict the access to the machine by requiring the use of a VPN service OR then do not make any of the data publicly available, but only share it with a limited set of users. Is not the option oasis:allowed_users: …
proposed_answer: With the setting, I cannot access the oasis at all, unless a user in this list is logged in Maybe I don't understand the question completely If I understood correctly, the question was more about controlling access without a special whitelist that would need to be maintained. title: DEV Deployment stalls on upload
question: I started the same upload again, it still took quite long but it finally passed. No idea what was going on between Friday and this morning. Typically when an upload hangs it is because of resources: the processing e.g. runs out of memory or crashes in some other unexpected reason. If possible, this should then be debugged locally. Also the `develop`-deployment is not really equitpped with very beefy machines, so trying to process anything very large there can be problematic. Are these all uploads that are left in a limbo state because of failed processing? They are not published or anything?
proposed_answer: yeah, they are all just failed processing, unpublished tests of mine. I think in the early days we did have the case that you describe above, where there were really some bugs in the e.g., parsers or GUI. But I haven't experienced anything like that in a long time. In all the cases I experience nowadays (and several others have had the same experience), something goes wrong on the infrastructure that causes the processing to hang. Reuploading the file a 2nd time results in successful processing. I do not think there is anything extraordinary about these uploads. The size may be larger than a typical DFT calculation, but in most cases uploads of this size are processing fine. It could be that there is some sort of memory bug or something that is subtle, but in my experience the hanging is somewhat rare and random, so I wouldn't really want to spend time digging into this. Perhaps we can start keeping a more detailed log of these issues to try to find any correlations. These are now removed

title: The need of strg+shift+R
question: what the expierence with nomad updates is. When i am updating plugins reloads are not helping but we have to do strg+shift+R. Is it possible to force a proper reload of nomad also when plugins have changes? is someone else experiencing that?
proposed_answer: There are two potential causes for things not updating. 1. The actual GUI code is only updated when the GUI has changed. There is a hash based on the GUI used as the name for the Javascript file that the browser is loading. Those are basically cached indefinetely. But this should not be a problem, has the GUI code has not changed. 2. The GUI uses a resource called `artifacts.js` and `env.js`. Those are dynamically generated by the nomad app at start-up time. The `artifacts.js` contains all the schemas and stuff. I assume this is what actually gives you trouble. There is caching on these, because they can be quite large. But the caching is supposed to be based on an "etag" which again is based on a hash of the `artifacts.js` content. This "etag" should change when you plugins change. Maybe something is wrong here. Your browsers network tab, you can see if a resource is loaded from cache or not. On the first reload after a change, you should see that the `artifacts.js` has actually been loaded from the server. In your network tab, you should see these response headers on the `artifacts.js`:

title: Quantities with experimental uncertainties
question: Is there currently a NOMAD way of saving a experimental measurement uncertainty assigned to a Quantity? (Eg. mean value +- std)
proposed_answer: If no, I would imagine this will be useful for everyone handling experimental data. Not yet, but on the PhysicalProperty discussion we are talking about these statistics For now we are adding a second quantity next to it. See for example: https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-material-processing/blob/main/src/nomad_material_processing/general.py#L573 My recommendation is: you can try it out now yourself, either following and Area A implementation, or doing it more abstract, following Area C current def of PhysicalProperty (https://github.com/nomad-coe/nomad-simulations/blob/develop/src/nomad_simulations/schema_packages/physical_property.py#L69). See the discussion in this issue: https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/issues/1368 From what I am reading here, I could imagine a ReferenceEditQuantity that is already using the form with `/` so that the normalisation does not have to add it later on. I leave this question for or others form the dev team. But I am int …
proposed_answer: This as issue with the nginx conf, the keycloak headers can be quite large: https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-distro-template/blob/main/configs/nginx_base_conf#L61 In the north nginx conf you can add the following lines to increase the default buffer size: ``` proxy_buffering on; proxy_buffer_size 16k; proxy_buffers 8 16k; proxy_busy_buffers_size 64k; ``` that did it.

title: Publishing DFT calculations from RSPt
question: If you are interested in this route, then I am happy to provide more guidance, just let me know your reply. I'll go point by point: > Can you give me a rough idea of the scope and user base of this code?
proposed_answer: > + > Then, when a RSPt parser is developed (we could even work together on this if your group uses this code a lot), your uploads can still be reprocessed for full search functionality. We will have to ask our partner for whom we are doing the publishing. We are doing it for a common EU project [MaMMoS](https://github.com/mammos-project), for which we wanted to use NOMAD as a platform to openly store and publish our data. Within this project we have all kinds of data, including ab initio, simulations and measurement data. Our partner who is working with this code are from Uppsala university. question: nomad_entries_v1 nomad_materials_v1 So the connection to Elasticsearch seems to be working, what could be the problem then? What could I check during the server startup process?
proposed_answer: The url could possibly be different, in `packages/nomad-FAIR/infrastructure.py` there's a function called `setup_elastic` Can you try printing out the auth and host? ``` def setup_elastic(): """Creates connection to elastic search.""" http_auth = None if config.elastic.username and config.elastic.password: http_auth = (config.elastic.username, config.elastic.password) global elastic_client print(http_auth, '%s:%d' % (config.elastic.host, config.elastic.port)) elastic_client = connections.create_connection( hosts=['%s:%d' % (config.elastic.host, config.elastic.port)], timeout=config.elastic.timeout, max_retries=10, retry_on_timeout=True, http_auth=http_auth, ) logger.info('setup elastic connection') from nomad.metainfo.elasticsearch_extension import ( create_indices as create_v1_indices, ) create_v1_indices() logger.info('initialized v1 elastic indices') return elastic_client ```

id: More a question than an issue: data curation.|2024-09-18T08:20:59.171000Z|31ca795375|q9-a10
title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
question: Data curation means that there is some control on the quality of the entries that are being generated, but NOMAD is not like that. In the central service we accept any kind of data. Now, an Oasis is a different thing: you can decide how to handle this. Now: > * Imagine you have a setup that directly outputs data to your NOMAD OASIS, can you then modify, delete, annotate the data sets, add some field to it etc.?
proposed_answer: Being admin of your oasis, you can essentially re-run/re-process entries at will (https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/staging/docs/howto/oasis/admin.html). You could have a script running at a different moment in time to run your entries to modify, delete, annotate fields... This can be done creating and maintaining a parser plugin. This is also how the central NOMAD works: we have some parsers that change over time, and we reprocess sometimes some of the entries present in the database (either to fix bugs, to populate new fields, or change some). > * Imagine you have an already published experimental dataset and you discover a calibration error. Can you correct it? And if yes, is there some kind of versioning, so that maybe people having used this dataset before see that what is in now is different from what they have used? My previous comment answers the first part of the question. Sadly, for the versioning part, no, we don't control the version of an entry in the database. So once you reprocess an entry, the information is overwritten and we don't keep any registry of the previous entries.
thread_id: More a question than an issue: data curation.|2024-09-18T08:20:59.171000Z|31ca795375

title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
question: 2 - Obviously the k-mesh will be completely wrong for non-uniform grids, but hopefully this should be fringe enough to not be a big concern, but another check that the k-points are uniformly spaced could be not that difficult? 3 - if the k_mesh.grid is provided but not the points, the points will be reconstructed (at least for Gamma-centered mesh) https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/blob/develop/nomad/normalizing/method.py?ref_type=heads#L378 `points = np.meshgrid(*[np.linspace(0, 1, n) for n in k_mesh.grid])`. This however will create the full grid, so again will be inconsisten …
proposed_answer: What I think we should do (we can take a stab at some of this, but at this point I'm looking for a agreement on prefered way to move forward here): Only construct the `k_mesh.grid` if either `all_points` or `points` and one of `multiplicities` or `weights` are provided (or points are provided and all mupltiplicities are one). In the second case we could use the normalized reciprocal cell, get list of symmetry operations and extend the points to all_points, while double checking that the weights/multiplicities match. And only from `all_points` we could try guessing the grid (preferably after also checking the uniform spacings). If multiplicities/weights are not provided, we just abort. IMO its better to provide no `k_line_density` value than a wrong value.

Depends: MongoDB does contain information that cannot be recreated by simply reprocessing (upload id, entry id, timestamps, authors, coauthors, inclusion in datasets, etc). If you need to restore this, you will need the MongoDB snapshot. But if you don't care, you can simply reprocess. proposed_answer: The way to recreate everythign with an existing ID would be to move the correctly named folder into .volumes, re-add the data (uploads + entries) into MongoDB from a backup, and run `nomad admin uploads index <upload-id>` to get ES populated with data about the entries.

title: Scatterplot: focus on region
question: so it looks like it does not attempt any filtering along the 2nd axis Yes, looks like somehow the filter for one of the axis is not set correctly, that is definitely a bug. Any idea what we should do in the case where we filter for a value in an array? Maybe we keep all the entries that contain at least one match in the array, but we should simply zoom in on the region and not show the rest of the points that did not match. Or do you think we should require all of the points in that array to match the criterion?
proposed_answer: Yes, the first would be the desired effect. Keep all entries that have at least one point in the desired region. But zoom in. I was able to fix the bug, but I think I cannot create a very good solution for the other problem. I think we have to separate the current "Focus on region" into two different actions: "Zoom" and "Filter". "Zoom" will simply zoom in on the selected region without affecting any of the filters. "Filter" would create filters from the current selection, and if "autorange=true", the plotted region changes accordingly. The reason why the "Filter" results may seem weird is the fact that filters simply return all entries where there is some data that fullfills the query. It does not care about where in the data the condition is filled, and will always just return the full entry. This is why if you create a filter based on `results.properties.catalytic.reaction.reaction_conditions.temperature` `results.properties.catalytic.reaction.reactants.gas_concentration_in`, the plot may also contain other reactions, simply because they are included in this entry where one of the reactions matched the query.

id: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing|2024-07-12T10:18:27.642000Z|c6b7f00525|q27-a28
title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
question: 1. There are actually 2 issues here: 1. One is where we read the k-points from the schema. Our new schema will always have a single storage place and *reference* it where necessary. In the old schema, it might be more interesting to give the parser control over which place to read from. 2. The other is *symmetry*. I think that unfolding the grid goes beyond the scope of a fix (though we may consider it as a feature in the future). I would see to leverage the *multiplicities* here. You mentioned that for OpenMX these would trivial to set (all 1 I assume)?
proposed_answer: 2. Fully agreed. I'll see to add this check. 3. If we fix point 1, wouldn't that also resolve this issue? 4. Very astute observation! I'd say it depends: if a 2D system is modeled in 3D, the extra dimension should not count. If it is truly 3D, then the dimension with only 1 k-point may indeed be the precision bottleneck, and `k_line_density` should point that out. Typically codes are pretty clear on a system's dimensionality, but some (e.g. VASP) always defaults to 3D, or the user does not leverage the dimension control well... Luckily we have a package that ascertains the dimensionality independently: MatID by Now it just becomes a question of the normalization order: `MethodNormalizer` has to come after. We might also call the analyzer 2x, but it's a pretty expensive routine. I've been working on supporting your feedback, especially the symmetry-reduced grid. I don't think that we can cover all cases with multiplicities (unless we unfold the symmetrized grid).
thread_id: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing|2024-07-12T10:18:27.642000Z|c6b7f00525

id: Reproducibility indicators bug from afm nexus file|2025-08-11T09:40:29.449000Z|631491f6f1|q33-a34
title: Reproducibility indicators bug from afm nexus file
question: DEBUG: NOT IN SCHEMA etc. Which is also true, as there is only the NXcollection defined there. So anything below is indeed not documented, and will not be parsed.
proposed_answer: If you want to make it available in Nomad, you would need to define possible reproducibility_indicators, either by adding them to the appdef, or by defining a base class for it The problem is related to the linking. Essentially, in `/NXafm/ENTRY/reproducibility_indicators`, we only define `cantilever_oscillator`, but none of its sub-items. NOMAD will then only try to parse the `cantilever_oscillator` group from the link, but not anything that is inside that Hdf5 group. This is the general case for links, if you want to have all sub-elements also show up at the link destination, you need to explicitly define them in application definition as well. If you run our HDF5 validator, you also get warnings like `Field /entry/reproducibility_indicators/cantilever_oscillator/phase_lock_loop/cantilever_phase_positioner has no documentation.`, meaning that this concept is not defined. NOMAD does not parse any elements that are not defined on the schema level. if it is important that these are parsed correctly (seems to be the case here) also in the `reproducibility_indicators` group, you need to add them to the application definitions on both sides of the suggested link hope that this was unde …

id: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing|2024-07-12T10:18:27.642000Z|c6b7f00525|q9-a10
title: k_line_density wrong in (not only) case of symmetry reduced k-grid - issues in k_mesh normalizing
question: 3 - if the k_mesh.grid is provided but not the points, the points will be reconstructed (at least for Gamma-centered mesh) https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/blob/develop/nomad/normalizing/method.py?ref_type=heads#L378 `points = np.meshgrid(*[np.linspace(0, 1, n) for n in k_mesh.grid])`. This however will create the full grid, so again will be inconsistent with the parsers which provide the symmetry reduced ones into `points` , so IMO we should save this one into `all_points` and duplicate them into points only in case of P1 symmetry. 4 - https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/n …
proposed_answer: Only construct the `k_mesh.grid` if either `all_points` or `points` and one of `multiplicities` or `weights` are provided (or points are provided and all mupltiplicities are one). In the second case we could use the normalized reciprocal cell, get list of symmetry operations and extend the points to all_points, while double checking that the weights/multiplicities match. And only from `all_points` we could try guessing the grid (preferably after also checking the uniform spacings). If multiplicities/weights are not provided, we just abort. IMO its better to provide no `k_line_density` value than a wrong value. Also some questions about my uderstanding of the metainfo in the end.

title: NotImplementedError for context.raw_path_exists method
question: When running nomad installation with app_worker, the `m_context` is an instance of the class `nomad.datamodel.context.ServerContext` which has a well-defined `raw_path_exists` method. But while running pytests, which uses the nomad as a package (and not the local installation), `m_context` is an instance of class `nomad.datamodel.context.Context` in which `raw_path_exists` is not implemented.
proposed_answer: Because of this difference, the pytests fail during the plugin testing, when using `archive.m_context.raw_path_exists(file_path)`. Can we harmonize these two methods for the classes `ServerContext` and `Context`? Clarification: when using pytest, `m_context` is of class `nomad.datamodel.context.ClientContext` which inherits `nomad.datamode.context.Context`, and as the child doesn't have its own `raw_path_exists` method, it defaults to the one from the parent, which is not implemented. Something like this could resolve it https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/commit/2e8d24cd139cef1a9b9b7244c25c00b4333f28f1

id: More a question than an issue: data curation.|2024-09-18T08:20:59.171000Z|31ca795375|q3-a4
title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
question: Because I'm mostly focused on formatting the data at the moment, I don't have a lot of experience with data curation. So here are for example a few more practical questions I was asked. - Imagine you have a setup that directly outputs data to your NOMAD OASIS, can you then modify, delete, annotate the data sets, add some field to it etc.? - Imagine you have an already published experimental dataset and you discover a calibration error. Can you correct it? And if yes, is there some kind of versioning, so that maybe people having used this dataset before see that what is in now is different from …
proposed_answer: - If you change affiliation, can you update it in your datasets? So yes, the question would be: what is possible? and until which level of sharing. (like how is the answer when the data is unloaded but not shared, how is it once it's shared, and how is it once it has a DOI for example). to comment on the "data curation" part. Data curation means that there is some control on the quality of the entries that are being generated, but NOMAD is not like that. In the central service we accept any kind of data. Now, an Oasis is a different thing: you can decide how to handle this.
thread_id: More a question than an issue: data curation.|2024-09-18T08:20:59.171000Z|31ca795375

id: More a question than an issue: data curation.|2024-09-18T08:20:59.171000Z|31ca795375|q12-a13
title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
question: Being admin of your oasis, you can essentially re-run/re-process entries at will (https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/staging/docs/howto/oasis/admin.html). You could have a script running at a different moment in time to run your entries to modify, delete, annotate fields... This can be done creating and maintaining a parser plugin. This is also how the central NOMAD works: we have some parsers that change over time, and we reprocess sometimes some of the entries present in the database (either to fix bugs, to populate new fields, or change some). > * Imagine you have an already published experimenta …
proposed_answer: My previous comment answers the first part of the question. Sadly, for the versioning part, no, we don't control the version of an entry in the database. So once you reprocess an entry, the information is overwritten and we don't keep any registry of the previous entries. I think this is a feature request. Maybe can tell you more, but for sure, we didn't start working on it. > * If you change affiliation, can you update it in your datasets? There is another issue regarding affiliation, but I don't know whether it is updated in the datasets. Good question, maybe knows 🙂
thread_id: More a question than an issue: data curation.|2024-09-18T08:20:59.171000Z|31ca795375

title: np.nan and None in nomad client
question: There is no way to tell whether a None represents nan or inf, when processing a given dict. And the typing system does not allow this beahviour due to data safety. What you can do: turn on `as_plain_dict=True` so that download as plain dicts.
proposed_answer: Work with plain dicts if possible. If you need EntryArchives, you need to manually replace affected nones with either nan or inf. Then call `m_from_dict`, reference code can be seen as attached. Ok, thank you for the input. I want to resolve references from my entry so the EntryArchive would be nice. title: np.nan and None in nomad client
question: When accessing this entry via API with requests everything works as expected. But when using the ArchiveQuery (`from nomad.client.archive import ArchiveQuery`) the `np.nan` becomes a problem. Because the error `TypeError: Cannot cast array data from dtype('O') to dtype('float64') according to the rule 'safe' ` does not let me download the entry. I was told that a related np.nan problem has been discussed here (https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/issues/2128). Can there also be a solution for the ArchiveQuery?
proposed_answer: There is no way to tell whether a None represents nan or inf, when processing a given dict. And the typing system does not allow this beahviour due to data safety. What you can do: turn on `as_plain_dict=True` so that download as plain dicts. Work with plain dicts if possible.

title: Protected Oasis - Restrict GUI/API Access only for Login-User
question: ``` oasis: is_protected: true ```to restrict the access on demand when deploying an oasis, if one need this extended authorization protection. What do you think?
proposed_answer: So far we have not thought about this kind of usage. Some people do limit access to the Oasis by only hosting it in an internal network which can be connected to only through e.g. a VPN + credentials. That seems like a very systematic way of limiting access. In principle what you suggest is doable, but we would need to investigate quite a lot of time to properly check and ensure that this flag correctly protects all of the endpoints etc. So I would say that at this point we are not willing to implement this, unless we hear from multiple sources that this would be a necessary feature. I would encourage trying out other solutions for now: restrict the access to the machine by requiring the use of a VPN service OR then do not make any of the data publicly available, but only share it with a limited set of users. Is not the option oasis:allowed_users: doing this?

title: More a question than an issue: data curation.
question: I was presenting FAIR data, FAIRmat and NOMAD yesterday, and I got a lot of questions about how to modify/remove data that has already been uploaded. Or the possibility to add more comments, or additional analysis to an entry. Because I'm mostly focused on formatting the data at the moment, I don't have a lot of experience with data curation. So here are for example a few more practical questions I was asked. - Imagine you have a setup that directly outputs data to your NOMAD OASIS, can you then modify, delete, annotate the data sets, add some field to it etc.?
proposed_answer: - Imagine you have an already published experimental dataset and you discover a calibration error. Can you correct it? And if yes, is there some kind of versioning, so that maybe people having used this dataset before see that what is in now is different from what they have used? - If you change affiliation, can you update it in your datasets? So yes, the question would be: what is possible? and until which level of sharing. (like how is the answer when the data is unloaded but not shared, how is it once it's shared, and how is it once it has a DOI for example). to comment on the "data curation" part.

title: Problems with data types after updating to NOMAD develop branch
question: ```I am now adjusting my file reader to explicitly convert the data to e.g. float for example above. But still I was wondering if NOMAD should not convert `'20'` into a float `20.0` as it did before. Maybe a guess, but could be worth a try, add a dot cuz the string "20" otherwise is unclear and maps more to an integer than a float, "20." is a different statement This is due to the new metainfo datatypes merged recently. And yes they are less forgiving now. Auto converting strings into numbers is really a stretch. This conversion from string to float is really the parsers job. whats your opinio …
proposed_answer: The type system internally tries to convert the input data into the target type. In this particular case, when a string "20" is given, the type instance tries to check the following: "20" == str(numpy.float64("20")), this results actual check to be something like "20" == "" due to the inexact nature of floats. The new system tries to be as smart as possible but for cases like this, the risk of loss of precision cannot be avoided. I'll say the easy fix is assign the correct value to the quantity, meaning that instead of "20", use numpy.float64("20"). for clarifying.
thread_id: Problems with data types after updating to NOMAD develop branch|2024-07-08T09:06:49.792000Z|923b00310a

title: Standard way of writing `m_proxy_value`
question: can you comment on this. I think the with `/` is the normalized form, right? Not sure why the ReferenceEditQuantity is doing it differently. I think Theodore gave the opinion that it was the intended behavior which I still don't understand why. See the discussion in this issue: https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/issues/1368 From what I am reading here, I could imagine a ReferenceEditQuantity that is already using the form with `/` so that the normalisation does not have to add it later on. But how big of an issue is this?
proposed_answer: Not sure. I haven't run into any problems recently but remembered a lot of headache from back when I wrote that issue 😅 in my function that compares the m_proxy_values (inside normalizer), I am adding / in the path before comparison. So for now, it works for as well. but it took some time to debug it given that one would not expect deviations in the form of the proxy value. I am afraid someone might run into this again in future 😅 Adding a leading / is part of the normalization so that we deal with a canonical form. What is happening at the front end is beyond me. This isn't super important at the moment, but maybe we can assign this issue to someone who is working in the frontend to work this out? 🙂

E.g. our user management and your oasis not running in sync. Your laptop being behind. When you open a new tab, you start a new instance of the gui and it will get a new (potentially out of time) token. So every time you open a new tab there is the potential for this to happen. Could you check your os time settings?
proposed_answer: I am not sure what the Gateway timeout problem is. Is the gateway timeout coming from keycloak or the nomad api? Perfect, I think the time issue was indeed the problem. I went to the OS settings and synchronized the clock again. From then on I did not yet get the validation error message again. This also explains why it sometimes worked and sometimes not, because from time to time the OS is synchronizing the clock automatically. Thank you very much. The Gateway Timeout Error is coming from the NOMAD API. But in the container logs there is no information about it. Maybe it was due to the Validation error, e.g. if I had no valid token, I was not allowed to access some entries and run into a timeout. I will keep an eye on that.

id: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files|2025-02-13T15:36:13.213000Z|31f7b310d4|q6-a7
title: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files
question: * Which version of Oasis and plugins are you using? * The ellipsometry example as it is shipped is tiny (provided it was not modified with own data) there might be parsing errors, in which case the logs record these issues but that should not make the system completely unresponsive. * Similar, uploads might get stuck sometimes but still the operation system remains operational, so in the case of the ellipsometry example, I guess you made an empty upload and dragged and dropped the NeXus file. Did this nevertheless then sent the server into frozen state?
proposed_answer: for your response, i will try to provide a more detailed account of our setup and tests: - I work with API calls made with a python script and the request package. I tried both creating an upload per file as well as using a PUT request to add files to an existing upload, both with the same result. I always make sure a file is fully processed before uploading the next file, either via checking the upload status (in a 3s loop, as suggested in the docs) or the 'wait_for_processing' parameter in the PUT request. - The .nxs files in question are converted from one of our laboratory instruments and all have 1.3MB. Even 200 such are much smaller than the 8GB RAM and 4GB swap memory we work with on the server. We do hit the main memory limit before utilizing the swap memory, but it never resets and eventually no swap memory is left and the freeze happens. At this point, we were not able to do anything but manually restart the computer via the power button, it does not respond to anything. - We are running Oasis version 1.3.13 and pynxtools version 0.9.3
thread_id: Oasis crashes when uploading multiple NeXus .hdf5 files|2025-02-13T15:36:13.213000Z|31f7b310d4

proposed_answer: yes here is what i get in terminal: The error indicates that you used a newer version of NOMAD and populated data, then switched to an older branch which has the older Elasticsearch version. This causes that error. The brute-force method of solving it is to clear all your local data with `docker compose down --volumes` but note that this will delete all of the stored data (in mongodb, elastic, etc) of your installation. yes, this solved the issue. proposed_answer: This Schema allows having NeXusMeasurements (e.g. Mpes) which are Measurements with an extra normalisation that sets the method used. The NexusMeasurements have Entries which are ActivitySteps. Note that I am normalising our Measurements so that `NexusMeasurement.steps` (inherited from `Measurement.steps`) hold the different ActivitySteps. question: I can show you how to integrate it in your scripts if you like There are two separate issues. As is correctly writing, the units are stored in the schema (i.e. the metainfo). I can understand the reason why someone want to have the unit there, but we cannot blindly repeat the unit in each archive, because NOMAD contains 24.5 billion individual quantities and repeating unit 24.5 times is quite expensive. For similar reasons, you won't find default values in the archive. Its not quite as obvious as makes it seem. What might be possible, is that we transparently add the unit information when the …
proposed_answer: Sometimes, you might need to store a unit in the archive, because you either do not know it when you write the schema or want to deviate from the unit in the schema. Here you would need to add the unit as a piece of data. Specifically for nexus and area B, we added a feature to the metainfo that allowed to specify that a quantity should have a `flexible_unit`. I am not sure if and how this is used. Mabye it needs to be generalized. Again this might be an issue where we could actually do something about. I cleaned a bit the file. This has a function to extract the unit from artifact.js Not storing units inside the RDMS as an optimization strategy is all good as the RDMS takes care of that contextualization. Export of contextualized pieces of information out of an RDMS be it for Oasis or for whichever RDMS is for which having serialization and contextualization export functionality available can be useful. This was my point. I probably do not understand the technical problem yet. From my (lay) perspective, an archive file contains a huge amount of information which to me seems superfluous. As an example I have a file with conductivity values for position x, y, these are stored in the …

id: How is it possible to describe ranged quantities?|2025-07-10T08:29:54.815000Z|271d44494a|q1-a2
title: How is it possible to describe ranged quantities?
question: I'm trying to describe some capabilities of various lab instruments in a nomad schema packages. In our lab we would like for example to describe the range of values available, for example of pressure in a process chamber. The current solution is to design the quantities for the min and max value and then searching them in the research app. In this way if an user will search for an equipment that works with an x value of pressure, it can be searched by imposing the mimum values of the entries less or equal to the value desired and the maximum value greater or equal to x. I hope that this could …
proposed_answer: I think a min and max quantity sounds like a great solution here. This reminds me also of the request for a ranges search widget we discussed at some point, e. G. To find all datasets covering a certain range of values in one property, e. G. An axis range this would be quite helpful, currently I have a similar workaround with two search widgets for min and max values In a scatterplot it is possible, If I understand what you are trying to do correctly. Such filtering also works with histograms, the issue is more that it is about ranges covered within a specific experiment, e.g. the energy range scanned in an experiment. The goal (at least for my case) would be to find all experiments which have data within a certain energy window, where each experiment has a range of energies. One can search for min and max of the energy range individually (and combining two searches also achieve the required), but not in one search widget. This is what in my view such a range search widget should do, i.e. search whether the selected range is inside the span (min->max). Not sure if this is easily doable in elasticsearch, though
thread_id: How is it possible to describe ranged quantities?|2025-07-10T08:29:54.815000Z|271d44494a
